title: alsine pinifolia
intro: Alsine pinifolia, also known as Alpine Sweethearts or Alpine Pink, is a small, flowering plant that belongs to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). It is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia, and is known for its pink or white flowers and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, coughs, and respiratory infections, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Alsine pinifolia can be used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to promote health and well-being, and are said to have antioxidant and immune-boosting effects.
